Category: --- COMPOSITIONAL: structural containment — what's inside what ---

Definition: Parent entity hierarchically contains child entities at any nesting level
Construction Examples: Project contains phases; phase contains work packages; building contains floors; floor contains zones; inspection contains checklist items; budget contains line items; schedule contains tasks

Definition: Entity is physically or logically made up of constituent parts
Construction Examples: Building system composed of elements; assembly composed of materials; work package composed of activities; contract composed of scope items

Category: --- CLASSIFICATION: how things are categorized and mapped to standards ---

Definition: Entity belongs to a category within a taxonomy or coding system
Construction Examples: Change order classified as design change; observation classified as safety hazard; cost classified under Division 26 Electrical; delay classified as weather

Definition: Entity maps to an equivalent in a different standard or classification system
Construction Examples: Company cost code maps to CSI MasterFormat division; field observation maps to OSHA hazard category; local spec section maps to industry standard

Definition: Entity has a specific type within its broader class
Construction Examples: Contract typed as subcontract vs. purchase order; change typed as owner-directed vs. design error; inspection typed as quality vs. safety; submittal typed as shop drawing vs. product data

Category: --- RESPONSIBILITY: who does / owns / is accountable for what ---

Definition: Work item or issue assigned to a responsible party for action
Construction Examples: Punch item assigned to drywall sub; RFI assigned to structural engineer; deficiency assigned to responsible contractor; action item assigned to project manager

Definition: Scope of work commercially contracted to a vendor or trade partner
Construction Examples: Electrical scope contracted to ABC Electric; steel supply contracted to fabricator; testing services contracted to third-party lab

Definition: Work physically executed by a trade or crew
Construction Examples: Concrete pour performed by concrete crew; wiring performed by electricians; waterproofing performed by specialty applicator

Definition: Entity overseen or administered by a person or role at the project or program level
Construction Examples: Project managed by project manager; program managed by owner's rep; safety managed by safety director; schedule managed by project controls

Definition: Entity examined or verified by a qualified party
Construction Examples: Fire stopping inspected by special inspector; air balance verified by TAB contractor; concrete strength verified by testing lab; punchlist walked by owner's rep

Definition: Entity created or specified by a design professional
Construction Examples: Structural system designed by structural engineer; MEP systems designed by MEP engineer; landscape designed by landscape architect; lighting designed by lighting consultant

Definition: Entity owned by a party who has decision authority over it
Construction Examples: Project owned by developer; asset owned by facility owner; specification owned by architect of record; change authority owned by owner

Category: --- FINANCIAL: how money flows through a project ---

Definition: Actual cost attributed to a budget category or cost account
Construction Examples: Rework cost charges against concrete cost code; change order costs against design contingency; direct cost charges against general conditions

Definition: Budget dollars contractually committed to a scope of work via contract or purchase order
Construction Examples: $2M committed to electrical subcontract; $500K committed to steel purchase order; $50K committed to testing services

Definition: Invoice or pay application submitted against a contract or budget line
Construction Examples: Monthly pay app billed against electrical subcontract; material invoice billed against purchase order; retainage release billed at substantial completion

Definition: Budget amount allocated for a planned scope or cost category
Construction Examples: $3M budgeted for structural steel; $200K budgeted for owner contingency; allowance budgeted for unforeseen conditions

Definition: Financial modification that changes an existing allocation or commitment
Construction Examples: Budget transfer adjusts concrete line item; change order adjusts subcontract value; cost reallocation adjusts contingency

Category: --- LIFECYCLE: how things evolve and transition through states ---

Definition: Entity evolves or transforms into a different entity type as it progresses
Construction Examples: RFI becomes change order; estimate becomes budget; punch item becomes warranty claim; proposal becomes contract; potential change becomes approved change

Definition: Entity has successive revisions tracked over time
Construction Examples: Drawing versioned as Rev C; schedule versioned as Update #12; specification versioned as Addendum 2; submittal versioned as resubmittal

Definition: Newer entity replaces an older one — the old entity is no longer current
Construction Examples: Addendum 3 supersedes Addendum 2; revised drawing supersedes original; updated schedule supersedes baseline; new spec section supersedes prior issue

Definition: Entity reaches a terminal state or disposition
Construction Examples: Observation resolves to closed; submittal resolves to approved as noted; RFI resolves to answered; punch item resolves to accepted; claim resolves to settled

Definition: Entity reviewed and approved with a specific status or conditions
Construction Examples: Submittal approved as noted; change order approved; pay application approved for payment; material sample approved; shop drawing approved with comments

Category: --- CAUSAL: where things come from and what they cause ---

Definition: Entity directly created as a result of another entity
Construction Examples: Change order originated from RFI; rework originated from design error; delay originated from failed inspection; claim originated from differing site condition

Definition: Entity caused by an external event or condition — root cause relationship
Construction Examples: Schedule delay triggered by material shortage; cost overrun triggered by scope creep; safety incident triggered by fall hazard; rework triggered by coordination failure

Definition: General association between entities without strict causation or hierarchy
Construction Examples: Coordination issue linked to RFI; observation linked to specification section; daily report linked to weather event; cost code linked to schedule activity

Definition: One activity or event occurs before another in time sequence
Construction Examples: Foundation pour precedes structural steel erection; submittal approval precedes material fabrication; rough-in precedes inspection; substantial completion precedes final payment

Definition: Entity cannot start or complete until a dependency is satisfied — blocking relationship
Construction Examples: Steel erection depends on anchor bolt survey; above-ceiling inspection depends on MEP rough-in complete; certificate of occupancy depends on all inspections passed

Definition: Activities or events occur during the same time period — parallel execution
Construction Examples: Electrical rough-in concurrent with plumbing rough-in; exterior enclosure concurrent with interior framing on lower floors; MEP coordination concurrent with structural design

Definition: One event is a predictive leading or lagging indicator of another — statistical relationship
Construction Examples: Quality observations lead punch items; RFI volume leads change order volume; submittal delays lead material delays; safety near-misses lead recordable incidents

Definition: Entity situated at a specific physical location within a project or site
Construction Examples: Deficiency located at Building A Floor 3; equipment operating at north tower; crew working at Zone B; material staged at laydown area

Definition: Entities in physical proximity — relevant for coordination and impact analysis
Construction Examples: Electrical conduit adjacent to plumbing pipe; excavation adjacent to existing structure; crane swing radius adjacent to occupied building; hot work adjacent to flammable storage

Category: --- RESOURCE: what resources are needed for and used by work ---

Definition: Entity needs a specific resource type to execute — demand relationship
Construction Examples: Concrete pour requires pump truck; steel erection requires tower crane and ironworkers; commissioning requires TAB contractor; paving requires compactor

Definition: Entity physically uses up a material or consumable resource
Construction Examples: Foundation pour consumes 200 CY concrete; painting consumes 150 gallons primer; waterproofing consumes 4000 SF membrane

Definition: Entity formally defines what a resource must be — product or performance requirement
Construction Examples: Specification section specifies concrete mix design; submittal specifies exact fixture model; drawing detail specifies connection type; performance spec specifies R-value

Definition: Resource assigned to a specific scope of work or time period
Construction Examples: 8 electricians allocated to Floor 7 this week; tower crane allocated to steel erection through March; laydown area allocated to curtain wall staging

Definition: Entity is a member of a group or organization
Construction Examples: Project manager belongs to general contractor; subcontractor belongs to project team; inspector belongs to testing agency; architect belongs to design team

Definition: Entity performs a specific role in a given context — same entity can play different roles across contexts
Construction Examples: Company plays GC role on Project A but subcontractor role on Project B; engineer plays designer on base bid but reviewer on change orders; superintendent plays safety officer on small projects

Definition: Hierarchical reporting or oversight relationship between parties
Construction Examples: Foreman reports to superintendent; superintendent reports to project manager; subcontractor reports to GC for schedule coordination; CM reports to owner

Definition: Entity involved in an activity or event without sole ownership
Construction Examples: Architect participates in OAC meeting; sub participates in coordination meeting; owner participates in punchlist walk; trades participate in safety standup

Category: --- DOCUMENT: how information and records relate to work ---

Definition: Document or record cites or points to another entity
Construction Examples: RFI references drawing sheet S-301; change order references specification section 05 12 00; daily report references weather conditions; submittal references spec section

Definition: File or media artifact physically associated with an entity
Construction Examples: Photo attached to observation; test report attached to inspection; shop drawing attached to submittal; signed lien waiver attached to pay application

Definition: Entity formally recorded or captured by a document or record
Construction Examples: Daily log documents field activity; meeting minutes document decisions; inspection report documents test results; as-built drawing documents final conditions

Definition: Document establishes binding rules or requirements for an entity
Construction Examples: Contract governs scope of work; specification governs material and installation; building code governs design; safety plan governs site procedures

Definition: Entity meets the requirements of a regulation or contractual standard
Construction Examples: Installation complies with building code; concrete mix complies with specification; safety procedures comply with OSHA standards; project complies with permit conditions

Definition: Entity fails to meet a regulatory or contractual requirement
Construction Examples: Fall protection violates OSHA 1926.502; fire stopping violates UL listing; installation violates approved shop drawing; work violates permit conditions

Category: --- RISK: how risks relate to project entities ---

Definition: Entity has a downstream effect on another entity's cost, schedule, scope, or quality — forward-looking consequence
Construction Examples: Design change impacts structural steel schedule and budget; material shortage impacts 3 trades and 12 schedule activities; foundation condition impacts project cost by $500K; permit delay impacts all downstream construction

Definition: Risk or exposure reduced by a control, action, contingency, or design decision
Construction Examples: Fall risk mitigated by guardrail installation; budget risk mitigated by contingency reserve; schedule risk mitigated by early procurement; weather risk mitigated by temporary enclosure; coordination risk mitigated by BIM clash detection

Definition: Activity, condition, or decision creates exposure to a risk — the source of risk
Construction Examples: Deep excavation exposes to cave-in risk; single-source material exposes to supply chain risk; concurrent trades expose to coordination risk; incomplete design exposes to change order risk; winter schedule exposes to weather delay risk

Category: --- MODEL: how digital representations relate to physical entities ---

Definition: Digital entity represents a physical building element, system, or condition — bridge between virtual and physical
Construction Examples: BIM wall element represents physical wall assembly; 3D model represents building design intent; digital twin represents as-built condition; point cloud represents existing site conditions; 4D model represents construction sequence

Definition: Two model elements occupy the same physical space — spatial conflict requiring resolution
Construction Examples: Duct clashes with structural beam; plumbing pipe clashes with electrical conduit; sprinkler head clashes with light fixture; curtain wall mullion clashes with slab edge; cable tray clashes with ductwork

Definition: Physical condition documented by a reality capture method — validation of current state against design intent
Construction Examples: As-built wall position captured by laser scan; construction progress captured by 360 photo; site topography captured by drone survey; facade condition captured by thermal imaging; underground utilities captured by ground-penetrating radar

Category: --- DOCUMENT: how information and records relate to work (continued) ---

Definition: Entity is a direct reply or answer to a question, request, or submission — conversational relationship
Construction Examples: RFI response responds to RFI question; submittal review responds to submittal submission; proposal responds to bid invitation; inspection correction responds to deficiency notice; correspondence reply responds to original letter

Definition: Permit, certificate, or authorization formally issued by a regulatory authority or authorized party
Construction Examples: Building permit issued by city building department; certificate of occupancy issued by code official; work permit issued by fire marshal; environmental permit issued by EPA; temporary certificate issued by AHJ

Definition: Entities share enough attributes to support meaningful comparison
Construction Examples: $50M commercial project comparable to similar projects in same region; subcontractor comparable to peers on similar scopes; schedule performance comparable across portfolio

Definition: Entity measured against a reference standard or population percentile
Construction Examples: Project rework cost benchmarked against industry median; safety incident rate benchmarked against company average; schedule variance benchmarked against portfolio norm

Definition: Metric or entity grouped by an analytical dimension for meaningful comparison
Construction Examples: Cost performance segmented by project type; safety metrics segmented by trade; quality defects segmented by building system; change frequency segmented by project phase
